title: Energy is no longer dead money — here's what investors are really buying
summary: For years, energy was easy to ignore — a laggard, a value trap, or at best a tactical bounce. But this time, the move in crude is colliding with a different setup in stocks.

title: Markets are reeling from high oil prices. But that doesn't mean more drilling.
summary: The war in Iran has sent oil futures soaring, but analysts say the run-up in prices is unlikely to translate to more drilling activity — at least in the short term.

title: Ministers warn petrol retailers against ‘unfair practices’
summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband called in forecourt operators in response to the Middle East crisis.

title: Ministers warn petrol retailers over prices in meeting
summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves has warned petrol retailers of a "shared obligation" to keep prices down for motorists amid soaring prices and war in the Middle East.  The Petrol Retailers Association (PRA) had threatened to pull out of the Downing Street meeting with Ms Reeves and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband hours before, citing "inflammatory language".  The meeting only went ahead after assurances from the Treasury that it would be held largely in private, with the PRA's Gordon Balmer saying the government did not apologise for its language.

title: Concorde Financial Exits Howard Hughes Holdings, Developer of Large Sun Belt Communities
summary: Howard Hughes Holdings owns large land holdings in fast-growing markets such as Las Vegas, Houston, and Phoenix, where it has developed master-planned communities over many years. Investors will watch whether the company can convert housing demand into both land sales and long-term retail, office, and mixed-use development.

title: CF Industries Is Up 76% and Fertilizer Supply Is Why
summary: The world’s largest producer of ammonia, CF Industries (NYSE:CF) shares hit an all-time high this week, up 67.6% year-to-date as escalating Middle East conflict tightened global nitrogen supply and sent fertilizer prices surging. CF is trading around $129.60 on Friday after pulling back from a $136 close on March 12. The thesis is straightforward right now, ... CF Industries Is Up 76% and Fertilizer Supply Is Why

title: What Comes Next After This Energy Stock Popped More Than 25% On A 'Tremendous Run'
summary: Exxon Mobil stock is riding the wave of rising prices for the energy market during conflict overseas. Where is it headed next?

title: Why Exxon and Energy Stocks Finally Started to Rise This Week as the Iran War Escalated
summary: Oil stocks were slow to make gains at the start of the Iran war but their price hikes have been moving more in line with the jump in oil prices this past week.  Occidental Petroleum Chevron  and  Exxon  were all on track to finish the week ending March 13 higher – by 6.3%, 4.3%, and 2.3%, respectively -early Friday, according to FactSet data.  Meanwhile, expected weekly gains for oil futures – 11.7% for  Brent crude  and 11.9% for  WTI  – were even bigger.

title: Oil Price Back to the Glory Days: Will XOM, EOG & COP Gain?
summary: Rising oil prices amid Middle East conflict put XOM, EOG and COP in focus as investors eye energy sector gains.

title: Here's How XOM is Actively Involved in Improving Air Quality Via CCS
summary: ExxonMobil advances carbon capture growth, launching a second CCS project in Louisiana and securing 3.2 MTA of CO2 across two active sites.
